fields.description: When disabled, all file URLs pointing to S3 or CDN will no longer resolve. Use the Rollback feature (Migration tab) to restore files locally before disabling.
fields.label: Enable S3 Integration

fields.description: CDN hostname used for public file URLs (e.g. https://cdn.example.com). Private file downloads use the S3 endpoint directly — presigned URLs cannot be proxied through Cloudflare.
fields.label: CDN URL

fields.description: Optional prefix added to every S3 key, e.g. production → files stored as production/public/…. Changing this after uploading files will break existing links.
fields.label: Folder Prefix

fields.description: When enabled, the browser uploads files directly to S3 via a presigned PUT URL — the Frappe server handles only signing, never file content.
fields.label: Enable Presigned Upload

fields.description: Default expiry for download presigned URLs. Also used as the minimum expiry for share links.
fields.label: Presigned URL Expiry (minutes)

fields.options:
How downloads work
All file downloads — public and private — go through Frappe for permission checks, then redirect the client directly to S3 via a short-lived presigned URL. File content never transits through the Frappe server.

Cloudflare users — if your CDN URL is proxied through Cloudflare, create a Cache Rule to bypass the cache for requests whose query string contains X-Amz-Signature. Without this, Cloudflare may serve a cached response with an expired signature to a different client.

Migrate existing files
Scans all File documents still pointing to local paths (/files/ or /private/files/) and uploads them to S3. The job runs in the background — progress updates every 10 files. Errors are logged per file without interrupting the job.

Restore files locally
Downloads all files currently on S3 back to your Frappe server and restores their original local paths. Run this before disabling the integration or uninstalling the app to avoid broken links.

Folder prefix change
When you save a new Folder Prefix value, Platine automatically starts a background job that:
  • Copies every S3 object from the old prefix to the new one
  • Deletes the originals from S3
  • Updates all public file URLs in the database
  • Updates platine_s3_key on every affected File document

The job cannot be undone. Run a Rollback first if you want to revert to local storage.

Safari compatibility — Safari's PDF viewer does not follow HTTP redirects inside embedded contexts (iframe, embed, object). When a private file is opened from within Frappe, Safari may display a blank preview instead of the file content.

To fix this, add the relevant MIME types below. When a request for a private file comes from Frappe itself (identified by the Referer header) and its type is listed here, Platine will stream the file content through the Frappe server instead of redirecting to S3 — no redirect, no Safari issue.

This only affects private files. Public files are always served directly from the CDN URL and are not impacted.

Browse common MIME types on MDN →

fields.description: One MIME type per line (e.g. application/pdf). Only applies to private files accessed from within Frappe.
fields.label: MIME Types to Stream

fields.description: Logs older than this number of days are automatically deleted by a daily job.
fields.label: Log Retention (days)

fields.description: Log every presigned URL generation for file downloads. High volume — disabled by default.
fields.label: Log Downloads

fields.options:
Transfer logs
All upload, delete, migration, rollback, CORS and connection events are recorded in Platine Log. Use the buttons below to view or clear them. CSV export is available natively in the list view.
